name: Richter Wogglebug
description: Random voltage generator after Grant Richter's Wiard Wogglebug. Voltage-controlled clock feeds a sample-and-hold, lag processor, gate-burst generator and two VCOs ring-modulated into each other. Seven simultaneous random/audio outputs.

controls.name: Ego/Id Balance
controls.description: Sets the range of probable random values when Ego In is unpatched. CCW clusters values near a narrow band. When Ego In is patched, balances external signal against the internal uncertainty source.
controls.detail: unpatched: probability range · patched: internal/external blend

controls.name: Woggle
controls.description: Sets how quickly the Woggle circuit catches up to the Smooth/Stepped CV. CW = sluggish follow; CCW = snappy chase.

controls.name: Speed / Chaos
controls.description: Dual purpose — sets Internal Clock rate AND the Smooth CV lag-processor response. CW speeds the clock and makes Smooth CV jittery; CCW slows the clock and smooths the response.

controls.name: Speed / Chaos CV Attenuator
controls.description: Unipolar attenuator for Speed/Chaos CV input. With nothing patched, extends internal clock upper range.

controls.name: Disturb Button
controls.description: Manual clock for the S&H. Press = sample new value; hold = pause CV outputs. At fast clock rates, kicks the system to fetch a fresh cluster of values.

inputs.name: Ego Input
inputs.description: External S&H source. Signal is injected directly into the Wogglebug's uncertain heart. Accepts CV or audio-rate signals.

inputs.name: Influence Input
inputs.description: CV or audio input to the Ring-Mod circuit. Also modulates Smooth/Woggle VCO frequency and level-shifts the Woggle CV.

inputs.name: External Clock Input
inputs.description: Any signal clocks the S&H when patched. Internal Clock Output keeps running at its own rate regardless.

inputs.name: Speed / Chaos CV Input
inputs.description: Unipolar control over Speed. Attenuated by Speed/Chaos CV Attenuator.

outputs.name: Smooth VCO Output
outputs.description: Sharktooth audio-rate waveform. Pitch controlled by Ego Input, Ego/Id setting, Influence CV and Speed/Chaos.

outputs.name: Ring-Mod Output
outputs.description: Pulse-wave audio-rate ring-mod product of Smooth VCO × Woggle VCO × Influence. Digital-flavoured, aggressive. Gets messy fast.

outputs.name: Woggle VCO Output
outputs.description: Square audio-rate signal. Pitch controlled by Woggle, Ego, Ego/Id, Influence and Speed/Chaos.

outputs.name: Stepped Output
outputs.description: Stepped random voltages. New value on every Internal Clock pulse (or External Clock / Disturb). At audio-rate clock, becomes bit-reduced noise. Activity window shows approximate voltage.

outputs.name: Smooth Out
outputs.description: Lag-processed (slewed) random CV. Smoothness set by Speed/Chaos — CCW = gentle smooth random, CW = audio-rate jitter.

outputs.name: Woggle CV Out
outputs.description: Product of Smooth and Stepped CV, shaken and chased by Woggle control. Always in motion.

outputs.name: Burst Output
outputs.description: Square random gate burst signal. Synced to internal clock, influenced by Stepped/Smooth/Woggle. Orange activity window shows gate activity.

outputs.name: Clock Output
outputs.description: Square clock from internal generator. Independent of External Clock Input — always runs at Speed/Chaos rate.

behaviors.name: Four CV flavours at once
behaviors.description: Stepped, Smooth, Woggle and Burst all derive from the same internal S&H but expose different characters simultaneously: Stepped = classic sampled random; Smooth = slewed/lagged version; Woggle = quivering product of the two; Burst = gate-burst rhythm. Pull all four for a coordinated random voice.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate clock → digital noise
behaviors.trigger: Speed/Chaos full CW · Speed CV near +8V
behaviors.description: Internal clock exceeds 200Hz. Stepped Output becomes bit-reduced noise, Smooth Out becomes jittery grainy CV, Clock Output becomes a square wave. Patch an audio signal to Ego In for sample-and-hold bit-crushing on that signal.

behaviors.name: Pitch-to-voltage following
behaviors.description: Patching a VCO or any audio signal to Ego In makes Woggle VCO Out and Smooth CV 'follow' the ego-in pitch. Ego/Id sets blend; Woggle sets the type/degree of following. Useful as a generative second voice that tracks a sequencer melody.

behaviors.name: Independent Master Clock
behaviors.trigger: patch anything to External Clock In
behaviors.description: External Clock overrides the S&H clock while Internal Clock Output keeps running at the Speed-set rate. This lets Wogglebug serve as both master clock source AND slave-sampling random generator from the same module.

behaviors.name: Disturb = freeze + step
behaviors.description: Press samples new values manually. Hold pauses CV outputs — Stepped, Smooth, Woggle all freeze for the duration of the hold. At slow clock rates, use Disturb as a performance-friendly next-value button.

behaviors.name: Influence warps pitch and ring-mod
behaviors.description: Influence simultaneously FMs both VCOs, feeds the ring-mod, and level-shifts Woggle CV. Patching a sequencer's pitch CV here yields discordant FM squelch that tracks the sequence. Patching LFO here animates the ring-mod timbre.

behaviors.name: Ego/Id probability distribution
behaviors.trigger: rotate Ego/Id with Ego In unpatched
behaviors.description: Ego/Id CCW makes random values 'cluster' around a narrow band — biased/coloured random. Fully CW spreads values across the full 0–10V range. Use for controlled pitch wanders vs wild jumps.

behaviors.name: Kick-the-Bug reset
behaviors.trigger: dummy cable → External Clock In · Speed 3+ o'clock
behaviors.description: A dummy cable (unplugged end) into External Clock defeats the internal clock without providing a new one. Press/hold Disturb to freeze last random values. Releases generate a fresh burst. Classic Wogglebug performance trick.

behaviors.name: Activity windows = value visualisation
behaviors.description: System Clock LED (blue) mirrors S&H clock rate — shows External Clock rate when patched, Internal rate otherwise. Stepped activity window shows approximate voltage. Burst orange window shows gate rhythm. Useful for dialling in without monitoring audio.

patchIdeas.name: Classic melodic random
patchIdeas.patch: Stepped Output quantized to pitch, Burst Output as rhythm trigger — a self-contained generative melody.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~12:00 (internal clock around a few Hz).
Set EGO/ID to ~10:00 (clustered values — narrow pitch range).
Leave EGO INPUT unpatched so the internal uncertainty source is used.
Patch Wogglebug STEPPED → quantizer IN.
Patch quantizer OUT → VCO V/OCT.
Patch Wogglebug BURST → envelope TRIG.
Patch envelope OUT → VCA CV, VCO OUT → VCA IN, VCA OUT → mixer.
Roll EGO/ID clockwise any time to widen the note range from clustered wander to full octave jumps.
patchIdeas.signalOut: STEPPED — 0 to 10 V stepped random CV, new value every internal-clock pulse, feeding the quantizer.
patchIdeas.hear: A self-playing pitched melody in whatever key the quantizer is set to, with rhythmic accents landing whenever the Burst output fires a flurry of gates.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~12:00
* Ego/Id: ~10:00

Quantizer:

- Wogglebug (Stepped) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Wogglebug (Burst) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bit-crushed S&H
patchIdeas.patch: Drive Ego In with an audio-rate signal while the clock runs above 200 Hz — Stepped Output becomes a bit/sample-rate reducer.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS fully clockwise (clock pushed past 200 Hz).
Set SPEED CV ATTENUATOR fully clockwise (unpatched +8 V normal lifts the clock to its highest range).
Set EGO/ID fully counter-clockwise (recommended in the manual for bit reduction).
Patch a VCO Saw or any audio signal → Wogglebug EGO INPUT.
Patch Wogglebug STEPPED → mixer / output (this is now your bit-crushed audio).
Slowly turn SPEED/CHAOS counter-clockwise to hear the sample rate drop — aliasing, warble, then chunks.
patchIdeas.signalOut: STEPPED — 10 Vpp sample-and-held copy of the Ego input, clocked at audio rates (up to ~200 Hz).
patchIdeas.hear: A gritty lo-fi version of the incoming oscillator: sweep SPEED/CHAOS and the tone moves from near-clean to aliased metallic warble to stuttering blocks of sample-held voltage.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: full CW
* Speed CV Att: full CW
* Ego/Id: full CCW

VCO:

- VCO (Saw) -> Wogglebug (Ego In)
- Wogglebug (Stepped) -> Output (In) // bit-crushed saw

patchIdeas.name: Pitch-to-voltage second voice
patchIdeas.patch: Feed an audio oscillator into Ego In so Woggle CV Out tracks — but quivers after — the source pitch.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~12:00.
Set WOGGLE to ~12:00 (medium chase speed).
Set EGO/ID to ~1:00 so the external signal mostly dominates the internal source.
Patch DPO VCO A Saw (or any audio-rate oscillator) → Wogglebug EGO INPUT.
Patch Wogglebug WOGGLE CV OUT → second VCO V/OCT.
Patch second VCO OUT → VCA → mixer alongside the DPO for a duet.
Rotate WOGGLE counter-clockwise for a snappier tracker, clockwise for a drifting ghost voice.
patchIdeas.signalOut: WOGGLE CV OUT — 0 to 10 V quivering CV that chases the Ego-input pitch, feeding the second VCO's V/OCT.
patchIdeas.hear: A second oscillator shadows the lead line but with wobble and hesitation — like a slightly drunken singer harmonizing, tightening or loosening as you turn WOGGLE.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Woggle: ~12:00
* Speed/Chaos: ~12:00
* Ego/Id: ~1:00

DPO:

VCO:

- DPO (VCO A Saw) -> Wogglebug (Ego In)
- Wogglebug (Woggle CV) p> VCO (V/Oct) // ghost harmony
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Jittery clock for MATHS
patchIdeas.patch: LFO into Speed CV In turns the internal Clock Output into a swinging/jittering master clock.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~11:00 (a moderate base rate).
Set the SPEED CV ATTENUATOR to ~12:00 so the CV influence is moderate.
Patch an LFO (slow triangle, ~0.2 Hz) → Wogglebug SPEED CV INPUT.
Patch Wogglebug CLOCK OUTPUT → MATHS CH 1 TRIG (set CH 1 as envelope, Cycle off).
Patch MATHS CH 1 OUT → filter CUTOFF (or any destination needing a clocked envelope).
Raise the Speed CV Attenuator for more pronounced rubato, lower for subtle swing.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CLOCK OUTPUT — 0 to +10 V square clock whose rate breathes with the LFO (independent of any signal at External Clock In).
patchIdeas.hear: A clock that speeds up and slows down with the LFO shape — tempo swells and contractions rather than a rigid grid, driving a predictable modulation that feels alive.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~11:00
* Speed CV Att: ~12:00

LFO:

- LFO (Out) >> Wogglebug (Speed CV In)
- Wogglebug (Clock) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Three linked random modulators
patchIdeas.patch: Stepped, Smooth and Woggle CV all share one internal clock — three flavours of the same random DNA modulating three destinations.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~11:00 (slow-ish clock).
Set WOGGLE to ~12:00.
Leave EGO INPUT unpatched; set EGO/ID to ~12:00 for full range.
Patch Wogglebug STEPPED → filter CUTOFF (sudden jumps).
Patch Wogglebug SMOOTH CV OUT → VCA CV (glide-in dynamics).
Patch Wogglebug WOGGLE CV OUT → VCO FM INPUT (quivering pitch drift).
Turn SPEED/CHAOS clockwise to tighten all three together; counter-clockwise to let them breathe.
patchIdeas.signalOut: STEPPED / SMOOTH CV OUT / WOGGLE CV OUT — three 0 to 10 V CV streams sharing one clock, sent to three destinations.
patchIdeas.hear: Stepped timbre jumps, Smooth volume swells, and Woggle pitch quivers — all synchronized yet different, so the patch breathes as one living modulation field.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~11:00
* Woggle: ~12:00

Filter:

VCA:

VCO:

- Wogglebug (Stepped) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Wogglebug (Smooth) >> VCA (CV)
- Wogglebug (Woggle CV) >> VCO (FM In)

patchIdeas.name: Influence-driven chaos
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer pitch CV into Influence In makes the Ring-Mod Output speak per-step, each step with a different angular timbre.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~12:00 (audible internal VCO range).
Leave EGO INPUT unpatched, EGO/ID to ~12:00.
Patch sequencer pitch CV → Wogglebug INFLUENCE INPUT.
Patch Wogglebug RING-MOD OUTPUT → filter IN (or straight to mixer).
Patch sequencer GATE → envelope TRIG → VCA CV, Ring-Mod through the VCA.
Tweak SPEED/CHAOS to move the internal VCO frequencies relative to the sequencer pitch — each knob position is a new chord of inharmonics.
patchIdeas.signalOut: RING-MOD OUTPUT — 10 Vpp ring-modulated pulse signal, timbre keyed to the Influence pitch CV.
patchIdeas.hear: Every step of the sequence becomes a different metallic/angular bell-like tone — dissonant, digital, but rhythmically locked to the sequencer.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~12:00

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) >> Wogglebug (Influence In)
- Wogglebug (Ring-Mod) -> Output (In) // angular per-step timbre

patchIdeas.name: Disturb-as-sample button
patchIdeas.patch: Clock effectively frozen, Disturb Button becomes a manual sample-and-hold trigger for whatever is at Ego In.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS fully counter-clockwise (internal clock approaches 1 minute/cycle — effectively off).
Patch an LFO (triangle, ~0.3 Hz) → Wogglebug EGO INPUT.
Patch Wogglebug STEPPED → filter CUTOFF (or VCO V/OCT through a quantizer).
Play a sound source through the filter.
Press the DISTURB BUTTON any time to sample the LFO's current phase into Stepped.
Hold DISTURB to freeze Stepped/Smooth/Woggle at their last values for as long as you hold.
patchIdeas.signalOut: STEPPED — 0 to 10 V, frozen between Disturb presses; each press captures the LFO's instantaneous value.
patchIdeas.hear: A hand-played sample-and-hold: silence-and-sudden-shift filter movements every time you tap Disturb, with full freeze when you hold the button down.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: full CCW

LFO:

- LFO (Out) >> Wogglebug (Ego In)
- Wogglebug (Stepped) >> Filter (Cutoff) // press Disturb to sample

patchIdeas.name: Generative drone (self-patched)
patchIdeas.patch: No external input — the Ring-Mod Output drones while the module's own CV outputs animate it.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~9:00 (slow clock — slow modulation).
Set WOGGLE to ~11:00, EGO/ID to ~12:00.
Leave EGO INPUT and INFLUENCE INPUT unpatched.
Patch Wogglebug RING-MOD OUTPUT → filter IN.
Patch Wogglebug SMOOTH CV OUT → filter CUTOFF.
Patch Wogglebug WOGGLE CV OUT → filter RESONANCE (or a second VCO FM).
Patch filter OUT → mixer. Let it run; slow SPEED/CHAOS further for longer evolutions.
patchIdeas.signalOut: RING-MOD OUTPUT — 10 Vpp angular audio, filtered and shaped by Smooth/Woggle CVs from the same module.
patchIdeas.hear: An evolving digital-tinged drone: angular ring-mod tone slowly opens and closes through the filter with Smooth, while Woggle adds restless shimmer on resonance.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~9:00
* Woggle: ~11:00

Filter:

- Wogglebug (Ring-Mod) -> Filter (In)
- Wogglebug (Smooth) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Wogglebug (Woggle CV) >> Filter (Res)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Burst-triggered drum rolls
patchIdeas.patch: Burst Output drives a drum, Stepped varies the accent — instant random fill generator.
steps: Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~1:00 (faster clock = denser bursts).
Leave EGO INPUT unpatched, EGO/ID to ~12:00.
Patch Wogglebug BURST OUTPUT → drum module TRIG.
Patch Wogglebug STEPPED → drum ACCENT (or pitch CV).
Monitor the drum's output.
Sweep SPEED/CHAOS to change roll density; the orange Burst activity window shows the rhythm.
Patch a slow LFO → Wogglebug SPEED CV INPUT for rolls that ebb and flow over time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: BURST OUTPUT — 0 to +10 V random gate bursts, synced to the internal clock; Stepped provides per-hit 0–10 V accent CV.
patchIdeas.hear: Stuttering random drum rolls — some tight, some scattered — with dynamic hits where Stepped happens to land high. Turn SPEED/CHAOS for the classic 'fill sweep' effect.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~1:00

Drum:

- Wogglebug (Burst) t> Drum (Trig)
- Wogglebug (Stepped) >> Drum (Accent)
- Drum (Out) -> Output (In) // chaotic drum roll

patchIdeas.name: Tame-the-Bug (external clock + slew)
patchIdeas.patch: External clock sets sample rate; Speed/Chaos now only sets Smooth's lag amount — gliding random pitch in the key of your master clock.
steps: Patch MATHS CH 1 (Cycle ON, slow — ~1 Hz square-ish) → Wogglebug EXTERNAL CLOCK INPUT.
Now Wogglebug's S&H is clocked by MATHS, so Speed/Chaos only affects Smooth-CV slew rate.
Set SPEED/CHAOS to ~11:00 (heavy lag — pitches glide).
Leave EGO INPUT unpatched, EGO/ID to ~10:00 for clustered wanders.
Patch Wogglebug SMOOTH CV OUT → quantizer IN, quantizer OUT → VCO V/OCT.
Patch MATHS CH 1 → envelope TRIG → VCA CV; VCO → VCA → mixer.
Turn SPEED/CHAOS clockwise to sharpen glides into near-stepped pitch changes.
patchIdeas.signalOut: SMOOTH CV OUT — 0 to 10 V slewed (lagged) random CV; new target on every MATHS tick, with the glide time set by SPEED/CHAOS.
patchIdeas.hear: A lazy, gliding random melody locked to MATHS' tempo — portamento between notes rather than jumps, tightening as you turn SPEED/CHAOS clockwise.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Richter Wogglebug:
* Speed/Chaos: ~11:00 (slew amount)
* Ego/Id: ~10:00

MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, slow

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) c> Wogglebug (External Clock In)
- Wogglebug (Smooth) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
